reports.raw_content:
November 24, 2025

Mary Pat Lee, Authorized Agent

Greater Minneapolis Crisis Nursery

4544 4th Avenue South

Minneapolis, MN 55419

License Number 830983 CRF

CORRECTION ORDER

Dear Mary Pat Lee:

On September 3-5, 2025, Department of Human Services (DHS) licensors conducted a licensing review at your facility, Greater Minneapolis Crisis Nursery located at 4544 4th Avenue South, Minneapolis, MN 55419. This review was conducted to determine compliance with state and federal laws and rules governing the provision of Children’s Residential Facilities under Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0010 through 2960.0120, and shelter care services under Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0510 through 2960.0530. As a result, DHS is issuing this order which requires you to take the correction action as described under each violation. Details of our findings are provided below. Our next steps and your options are also detailed.

LICENSING VIOLATIONS

DHS determined that your program failed to follow licensing rules and statutes, as described below.

Policies, Procedures and Practices

1. Violation: A copy of the resident rights was not posted at the staff workstation.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0050, subpart 3, item D.

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same Rule.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the resident rights are posted and meet all applicable requirements. This violation was corrected during the licensing review. No further corrective action is required.

2. Violation: The physical environment was not maintained on a consistent, healthy basis. During an on-site licensing review, DHS Licensors observed walls in need of repair, mold, baseboard radiators with exposed heating elements, and leaking duct work and lights.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0110, subpart 3, item A.

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same Rule.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the physical environment and equipment meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a written response and photographs demonstrating that the mold has been mitigated, walls have been repaired, and baseboard radiator heating is not accessible by residents served.

3. Violation: The license holder’s response to 1 of 13 grievances reviewed did not meet requirements. There was no documentation to demonstrate the license holder responded to a grievance dated March 31, 2025.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 18, item A.

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same rule.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that grievance policy meets all applicable requirements.

4. Violation: The license holder did not meet requirements for crib safety in the following ways:

a. There was no documentation to demonstrate monthly crib inspections had been completed for calendar year 2023; and

b. The license holder was using a non-full-sized crib mattress that was significantly smaller than the frame of the non-full-sized crib, leaving a gap larger than one inch at the perimeter.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.146, subdivision 4, paragraph (a).

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same statute.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure compliance with crib safety requirements. The license holder did immediately dispose of the ill-fitting crib mattress.

Personnel

5. Violation: Three of fifteen personnel files reviewed for requirements governing medication assistance did not meet requirements (personnel files numbered 2, 4 and 8). There was no documentation to demonstrate the staff person was trained to administer medications.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 11, item D.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure medication assistance training meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit the following:

· A revised health and hygiene services policy that includes training by a medically licensed person for facility staff responsible for medication assistance;

· Documentation identifying all staff who administer medications;

· Documentation demonstrating they have received the required training.

6. Violation: Thirteen of thirteen personnel files reviewed did not meet requirements governing the license holder’s compliance with an approved variance dated 12/4/2006 allowing unlicensed staff people to conduct health screens (personnel files numbered 1 through 13). There was no documentation to demonstrate the license holder complied with the variance condition that unlicensed staff people would be trained by a qualified health professional to conduct health screens.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Statute, section 245A.04, subdivision 9.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure compliance with approved variances. Within 30 days, submit documentation demonstrating all staff who conduct health screens have been trained by a qualified health professional.

7. Violation: Eight of eight personnel files reviewed for requirements governing orientation training did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. Orientation to cultural diversity and gender sensitivity, general and special needs and operational policies and procedures was completed late, after first contact with residents (personnel file numbered 1);

b. There was no documentation to demonstrate the following required training was completed:

i. General and special needs of residents served (personnel files numbered 11-15);

ii. Data practices and regulations (personnel files numbered 1, 11-15);

iii. Administration of screenings upon admission (personnel files numbered 1, 11-15);

iv. The use of time-outs (personnel files numbered 1, 11-15);

v. The drug and alcohol policy (personnel files numbered 9, 11 and 12); and

vi. Staff duties in implementing the program’s policies and procedures (personnel files numbered 1, 9-15); and

c. Documentation of training did not include the name of the entity providing the training (personnel files numbered 1, 9-15).

Statute and Rules Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.04, subdivisions 1, paragraph (c) and 14, paragraph (b); Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0100, subpart 3, item A; 2960.0070, subpart 5 and 2960.0080, subpart 5, item D.

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same rule.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that orientation training meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit documentation demonstrating compliance for personnel files numbered 1, 9-15.

8. Violation: Ten of ten personnel files reviewed for requirements governing ongoing training did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. The annual training on maltreatment of minors reporting requirements and definitions was completed late:

i. Calendar year 2023 (personnel file numbered 3);

ii. Calendar year 2025 (personnel files numbered 12 and 15);

b. There was no documentation to demonstrate the staff person received training on maltreatment of minors reporting requirements and definitions in 2023 (personnel file numbered 8);

c. There was no documentation to demonstrate the staff person was trained on the risk of sudden unexpected infant death (personnel files numbered 2 through 5, 8 and 15);

d. The license holder did not review the emergency plan with staff at least once every six months (personnel files numbered 2 through 5, 8, 10 through 12, 14 and 15).

Statute and Rule Violated: Minnesota Statutes, sections 245A.66, subdivision 4; 245A.1444 and Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 14.

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on January 14, 2022, you were previously found in violation of this same statute and rule.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that ongoing training meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit documentation demonstrating the following:

· The staff persons identified above have reviewed the emergency plan.

· The staff persons identified above have been trained on the risk of sudden unexpected infant death.

Residents:

9. Violation: One resident file (resident file numbered 3) reviewed for requirements governing time out procedures did not meet requirements. Documentation of a time-out implemented on 7/5/2025 did not include information about the resident’s ability to maintain acceptable behavior after the time-out was not included in the documentation.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 5, item D.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that time-outs/Separation procedures meet all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit documentation of a time-out procedure that demonstrates compliance.

10. Violation: One of three (resident file numbered 1) resident files reviewed for requirements governing discharge procedures did not meet requirements. The belongings inventory was not initialed by the parent upon receipt of belongings.

Rule Violated: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0090, subpart 3.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that discharge procedures meet all applicable requirements.

Written Response Required

If you fail to correct the violation(s) specified in the Correction Order within the prescribed time lines the Commissioner may issue an Order of Conditional License or may impose a fine and order other licensing sanctions pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, sections 245A.06 and 245A.07.

Submissions required as part of the corrective action ordered must be sent to your licensor by email at Laura.Huff1@state.mn.us or by mail:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Laura Huff

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

YOUR RIGHT TO REQUEST RECONSIDERATION

You have the right to request reconsideration of this order and the cited violations. Your request must:

· Be in writing

· List each violation you are challenging and identify what is inaccurate or incomplete about the information in the order

· Supply information that is accurate or more complete

· Be made before the deadlines provided below

If you are mailing your request, it must be received by DHS within 20 calendar days from when you received this order. If you do not meet this deadline, you lose your right to request reconsideration. The timeline to appeal began when you received this order. Please send it to:

Office of Inspector General

Legal Counsel’s Office

Attn: Licensing Legal Unit

PO Box 64953

St. Paul, MN 55164-0953

If your request is being personally delivered, it must be received by DHS within 20 calendar days from when you received this order. Please bring it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General, Legal Counsel’s Office - Licensing

444 Lafayette Road North

St. Paul, MN 55155

Legal authority

This action is taken under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, subdivision 1. The timeline to request reconsideration of the order is provided in Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, subdivision 2.

Questions

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, you may contact me at 651-431-4234 or at Laura.Huff1@state.mn.us.

Sincerely,

Laura Huff, Licensor

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer

reports.raw_content:
November 7, 2025                  CERTIFIED MAIL

Mary Pat Lee, Authorized Agent

Greater Minneapolis Crisis Nursery

4544 4th Ave. S

Minneapolis, MN 55419

License Number: 830983 (CRF)

ORDER TO PAY A FINE FOR

BACKGROUND STUDY VIOLATION(S)

Dear Mary Pat Lee:

The Department of Human Services (DHS) is ordering you to pay a fine of $2600 for 13 background study violations. Details of our findings are provided below. Our next steps and your options are also detailed.

Reason for the Fine

DHS is issuing this fine order based on your failure to comply with the following background study requirement(s):

Background Study Violations Determined on September 4, 2025:

1. As a result of a licensing review, a DHS licensor determined that the license holder did not remove staff persons who did not cooperate with the background study in the following ways:

· A background study was submitted by the license holder but the staff person did not provide fingerprints within the time required (SP1, SP2, SP3, SP4 and SP8) and

· A background study was submitted by the license holder but the staff person did not provide consent within the time required (SP5 and SP10).

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245C.09, subdivision 1

Fine: $1400 ($200 per violation)

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, you must comply with the background study requirements in Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245C. In response to this violation, the license holder has received cleared background studies for SP1, SP4, SP5, SP8 and SP10. The study for SP2 has required more time and SP3 has since been terminated.

2. As a result of a licensing review, a DHS licensor determined that you did not initiate a background study under Minnesota Statute 245C.03 subdivision1 for one staff person (SP11) before they began working in a position allowing direct contact with, or access to, persons served by the program.

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245C.04, subdivision 1, paragraph (h).

Fine: $200

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, you must comply with the background study requirements in Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245C. The license holder initiated a background study in response to this violation on 9/8/2025 and received clearance for SP11 to work in this facility on 9/13/2025. Immediately and on an ongoing basis, you must comply with the background study requirements in Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245C.

3. As a result of a licensing review, a DHS licensor determined that the license holder did not submit a background study request to DHS for an individual who previously had a cleared study that expired for the following (SP6, SP7, SP9, SP12 and SP13):

· SP6 began working in a position allowing direct contact with persons served by the program on 11/05/2012. However, the license holder had not initiated a background study on SP6 in NETStudy 2.0 at the time of the licensing review.

· SP7 began working in a position allowing direct contact with persons served by the program on 05/21/2011. However, the license holder had not initiated a background study on SP7 in NETStudy 2.0 at the time of the licensing review.

· SP9 began working in a position allowing direct contact with persons served by the program on 10/1/2015. However, the license holder had not initiated a background study on SP9 in NETStudy 2.0 at the time of the licensing review.

· SP12 began working in a position allowing direct contact with persons served by the program on 09/15/2004. However, the license holder had not initiated a background study on SP12 in NETStudy 2.0 at the time of the licensing review.

· SP13 began working in a position allowing direct contact with persons served by the program on 04/30/2008. However, the license holder had not initiated a background study on SP13 in NETStudy 2.0 at the time of the licensing review.

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245C.03, subdivision 1 and 245C.04, subdivision 1, paragraph (h).

Fine: $1000 ($200 per violation)

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, you must comply with the background study requirements in Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245C.

Billing and Payment of the Fine

DHS will send you an invoice for the $2600 fine. Payment must be made as directed on the invoice.

If you request a contested case hearing, as described below, do not pay the fine at this time. Following the contested case hearing, the Commissioner of DHS will issue a final order on this matter.

Please note, you may not avoid payment of this fine by closing, selling, or otherwise transferring the license to a third party. If this occurs, you are personally responsible for payment.

Your Right to Appeal

You have the right to appeal the fine. Your request must be in writing and clearly state that you are requesting a contested case hearing for this matter. Your request must be made before the deadlines provided below. If you do not meet this deadline, you lose your right to an administrative appeal. The timeline to appeal began when you received this order.

If you are mailing your request, it must be sent by certified mail and postmarked within 10 calendar days from when you receive this order. Please send it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General

Licensing Division

Attention: Legal Unit

PO Box 64953

St. Paul, MN 55164-0953

If your request is being personally delivered, it must be received by DHS within 10 calendar days from when you receive this order. Please bring it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General

Licensing Division

Attention: Legal Unit

444 Lafayette Road North

St. Paul, MN 55155

Upon DHS’ receipt of your timely appeal, your case would be scheduled for a contested case hearing in front of an Administrative Law Judge. Following this hearing, the Commissioner of DHS will issue a final order.

Legal representation at the contested case hearing

You do not need a lawyer to appeal. However, a lawyer can help you with your appeal. The state or county will not get you a lawyer and will not pay for a lawyer. If you cannot afford a lawyer, you may be able to get free legal advice or help with your appeal. To find out if free help is available, contact: Volunteer Lawyers Network at 612-752-6677; Central Minnesota Legal Services at 612-332-8151; Southern Minnesota Legal Services at 651-222-4731; or go to www.justice4mn.org to find a local legal services program that may be able to help you.

You can also find information on contested cases from the Office of Administrative Hearings website at https://mn.gov/oah/self-help. Click on Administrative Law Overview, then click on Administrative Law Contested Case Hearing Guide for a list of frequently asked questions.

Legal Authority for this Licensing Action

· This action is taken under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 1, paragraph (a), which describes under which conditions DHS may impose a fine against a license.

· DHS may impose a $200 fine for each background study violation under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (c)(4)(iv).

· The exception that allows a license holder who self-corrects a background study violation to avoid a fine does not apply when the license holder was cited for the same violation within the past 365 days, under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (d).

· Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (c)(5) states that license holders may not avoid payment of a fine by closing, selling, or transferring a license.

· License holders have a right to appeal licensing actions and request a contested case hearing, under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 14 and Minnesota Rules, parts 1400.8505 to 1400.8612.

Questions

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, you may contact Katie Leuer, Supervisor, at 651-431-6259.

Sincerely,

Paula Halverson, Unit Manager

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer

reports.raw_content:
April 24, 2024

Rachael Swanson, Authorized Agent

Hazelden Plymouth

15251 Pleasant Valley Rd., PO BOX 11, FO-1

Center City, MN 55012

License Number: 1038741

Report Number: 202308285

CORRECTION ORDER

Dear Rachael Swanson:

A licensing investigation of Hazelden Plymouth, located at 11505 36th Ave N., Plymouth, MN 55441, was conducted to determine compliance with state and federal laws and rules governing the provision of children’s residential facilities under Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0010 through 2960.0120, children's group residential facilities under Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0130 through 2960.0220, and chemical dependency services under Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0430 through 2960.0490. As a result of this licensing investigation a Correction Order is being issued.

A.   Reason for Correction Order

Pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, if the Commissioner of the Department of Human Services (DHS) finds that an applicant or license holder has failed to comply with an applicable law or rule and this failure does not imminently endanger the health, safety, or rights of the persons served by the program, the Commissioner may issue a Correction Order to the applicant or license holder.

The following violations of state or federal laws and rules were determined as a result of the licensing review. Corrective action for each violation is required by Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06 and is hereby ordered by the Commissioner of Human Services.

1. Citation: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.65, subdivision 2.

Violation: The license holder’s program abuse prevention plan (PAPP) did not meet requirements. The license holder’s Children’s Residential Facility was co-located to their licensed Detoxification Services and Substance Use Disorder Treatment programs, which were licensed to serve adults, and a Facility Abuse Prevention Plan addressed all three licensed programs:

a.  The PAPP did not include measures to limit the possibility of harmful substances being brought into the facility at admission or accessed by residents in shared public spaces, such as bathrooms, visiting areas, and the cafeteria; and

b.  The PAPP did not include measures providing more frequent supervision of children who display behaviors that pose an imminent risk of harm.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the program abuse prevention plan (PAPP) meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a revised program abuse prevention plan (PAPP) that meet requirements to demonstrate compliance.

2. Citation: Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0160, subpart 2.

Violation: The resident (R1) file did not contain documentation demonstrating the license holder determined whether the resident is a danger to themselves or others.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the documentation of admission criteria meets all applicable requirements.

3. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0090, subpart 2.

Violation: The no-eject discharge documentation for a resident (R1) did not meet requirements. The documentation did not demonstrate that all identified persons of the resident’s treatment team were included to develop any additional strategies to resolve the issues leading to discharge to allow the resident an opportunity to continue receiving services from the license holder.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the documentation of a no-eject discharge meets all applicable requirements.

If you fail to correct the violations specified in the Correction Order within the prescribed time lines the Commissioner may issue an Order of Conditional License or may impose a fine and order other licensing sanctions pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, sections 245A.06 and 245A.07.

Submissions required as part of a corrective action ordered must be sent to DHS Licensing at:

1. By secure email to: tina.christensen@state.mn.us; or

2. By mail to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Tina Christensen

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

B.   Right to Request Reconsideration

If you believe any of the citations are in error, you have the right to request that the Commissioner of Human Services reconsider the parts of the Correction Order that you believe to be in error. The request for reconsideration must be in writing and received by the Commissioner within 20 calendar days after receipt of this report. Your request for reconsideration must be sent to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Legal Unit

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

Please note that a request for reconsideration does not stay any provisions or requirements of the Correction Order. The Commissioner’s disposition of a request for reconsideration is final and not subject to appeal under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 14.

If you have any questions regarding this Correction Order, please contact me as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Tina Christensen, MH/SUD/CRF Senior Licensor

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

651-431-6610

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer

October 25, 2023                  CERTIFIED MAIL

Rachael Swanson, Authorized Agent

Hazelden Plymouth

15251 Pleasant Valley RD

PO Box 11 F0-1

Center City, MN 55012

License Number: 1038741 (CRF)

ORDER TO PAY A FINE FOR

BACKGROUND STUDY VIOLATION

Dear Rachael Swanson:

The Department of Human Services (DHS) is ordering you to pay a fine of $2,200 for 11 background study violations. Details of our findings are provided below. Our next steps and your options are also detailed.

Reason for the Fine

DHS is issuing this fine order based on your failure to comply with the following background study requirement:

Background Study Violation Determined on August 21, 2023:

1. As a result of a licensing review a DHS licensor determined that you failed to request a background study for eleven staff persons before they began working in a position before an adult, regardless of whether or not the individual will have direct contact with persons began working in the facility or setting.

A staff person (SP) was hired and began working in a position with persons served by the program. At the time of the licensing review on August 21, 2023, the license holder had not submitted the required background study request to DHS for the following staff working in a children’s residential facility:

a. SP1 began a position working in the facility August 2, 2023;

b. SP2 began a position working in the facility October 24, 2022;

c. SP3 began a position working in the facility June 26, 2023;

d. SP4 began a position working in the facility October 17, 2022;

e. SP5 began a position working in the facility March 6, 2023;

f. SP6 began a position working in the facility May 23, 2022;

g. SP7 began a position working in the facility January 31, 2022;

h. SP8 began a position working in the facility June 5, 2023;

i. SP9 began a position working in the facility April 3, 2023;

j. SP10 began a position working in the facility June 11, 2018; and

k. SP11 began a position working in the facility February 27, 2023.

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, , and 245C.04, subdivision 11.

Fine: $2,200 (eleven violations at $200 per violation)

Corrective Action Required: You must comply with background study requirements in Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245C. Within 10 days from receipt of this order, submit written documentation to your licensor detailing how compliance will be maintained in the future.

Billing and Payment of the Fine

DHS will send you an invoice for the $2,200 fine. Payment must be made as directed on the invoice.

If you request a contested case hearing, as described below, do not pay the fine at this time. Following the contested case hearing, the Commissioner of DHS will issue a final order on this matter.

Please note, you may not avoid payment of this fine by closing, selling, or otherwise transferring the license to a third party. If this occurs, you are personally and jointly responsible for payment. If you do not pay the fine on or before the date specified on the invoice and you did not request a contested case hearing, as described below, the Commissioner may issue a second fine, may not issue or reissue a license, or may suspend the license until the license holder pays the fine.

Your Right to Appeal

You have the right to appeal the fine. Your request must be in writing and clearly state that you are requesting a contested case hearing for this matter. Your request must be made before the deadlines provided below. If you do not meet this deadline, you lose your right to an administrative appeal. The timeline to appeal began when you received this order.

If you are mailing your request, it must be sent by certified mail and postmarked within 10 calendar days from when you receive this order. Please send it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General

Legal Counsel’s Office

Attn: Licensing Legal Unit

PO Box 64953

St. Paul, MN 55164-0953

If your request is being personally delivered, it must be received by DHS within 10 calendar days from when you receive this order. Please bring it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General

Legal Counsel’s Office

Attn: Licensing Legal Unit

444 Lafayette Road North

St. Paul, MN 55155

Upon DHS’ receipt of your timely appeal, your case would be scheduled for a contested case hearing in front of an Administrative Law Judge. Following this hearing, the Commissioner of DHS will issue a final order.

Legal representation at the contested case hearing

You do not need a lawyer to appeal. However, a lawyer can help you with your appeal. The state or county will not get you a lawyer and will not pay for a lawyer. If you cannot afford a lawyer, you may be able to get free legal advice or help with your appeal. To find out if free help is available, contact: Volunteer Lawyers Network at 612-752-6677; Central Minnesota Legal Services at 612-332-8151; Southern Minnesota Legal Services at 651-222-4731; or go to www.lawhelpmn.org to find a local legal services program that may be able to help you.

You can also find information on contested cases from the Office of Administrative Hearings website at https://mn.gov/oah/self-help. Click on Administrative Law Overview, then click on Administrative Law Contested Case Hearing Guide for a list of frequently asked questions.

Legal Authority for this Licensing Action

· DHS may impose a $200 fine for each background study violation under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (c)(4)(iv).

· Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (c)(5) states that license holders may not avoid payment of a fine by closing, selling, or transferring a license.

· Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.04, subdivision 7, paragraph (d)(4), states that DHS shall not issue or reissue a license if the applicant, license holder, or controlling individual has an outstanding debt related to a license fee, licensing fine, or settlement agreement for which payment is delinquent.

· Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.07, subdivision 3, paragraph (c)(2), states that that the license holder shall pay the fine assessed on or before the payment date specified, and if the license holder does not do so the commissioner may issue a second fine or suspend the license until the license holder complies.

· License holders have a right to appeal licensing actions and request a contested case hearing, under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 14 and Minnesota Rules, parts 1400.8505 to 1400.8612.

Questions

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, you may contact Katie Leuer, Supervisor, at 651-431-6259.

Sincerely,

Paula Halverson, Unit Manager

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer
https://mn.gov/dhs/general-public/licensing/
reports.summary: The Department of Human Services (DHS) is ordering you to pay a fine of $2,200 for 11 background study violations. Details of our findings are provided below. Our next steps and your options are also detailed.

reports.raw_content:
October 24, 2023

Rachael Swanson, Authorized Agent

Hazelden Plymouth

15251 Pleasant Valley RD

PO Box 11 F0-1

Center City, MN 55012

License Number: 1038741 (CRF)

CORRECTION ORDER

Dear Rachael Swanson,

On August 21 through 24, 2023 a licensing review of Hazelden Plymouth located at 11505 36th Ave N, Plymouth, Minnesota 55441 was conducted to determine compliance with state and federal laws and rules governing the provision of children’s residential facilities under Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0010 through 2960.0120, group residential services under Minnesota Rules, 2960.0130 through 2960.0220, and chemical dependency services under Minnesota Rules, 2960.0430 through 2960.0490 (Rule CRF). As a result of this licensing review a Correction Order is being issued.

A. Reason for Correction Order

Pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, if the Commissioner of the Department of Human Services (DHS) finds that an applicant or license holder has failed to comply with an applicable law or rule and this failure does not imminently endanger the health, safety, or rights of the persons served by the program, the Commissioner may issue a Correction Order to the applicant or license holder.

The following violation(s) of state or federal laws and rules were determined as a result of the licensing review. Corrective action for each violation is required by Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06 and is hereby ordered by the Commissioner of Human Services.

Policies, Procedures, and Practices

1. Citation: Minnesota Statutes 245A.04, subdivision 14, paragraph (a) and Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0150, subpart 3, item G.

Violation: The license holder’s Staffing Plan does not identify how the direct care staff to resident supervision ratios required for CRF residents will be met.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the Staffing Plan meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a revised Staffing Plan that meets all applicable requirements to demonstrate compliance.

2. Citation: Minnesota Rules 2960.0450, subpart 2, item D and subpart 4.

Violation: The license holder’s implementation of Therapeutic Recreation treatment services did not meet requirements. Services were not led by, directed by, or provided according to a plan developed by a qualified alcohol and drug counselor.

Correction Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the treatment services provided meet all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a Therapeutic Recreation plan that meets all applicable requirements to demonstrate compliance.

3. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0060, subparts 3, item A.

Violation: The license holder’s program evaluation did not meet requirements. An annual evaluation of the strengths and weaknesses of the program was not completed for calendar years 2021 and 2022.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that outcome measures and evaluations meet all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

4. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 17, item A.

Violation: The license holder’s reporting of critical incidents did not meet requirements. Twelve of fifty-six incidents reviewed met the definition of a critical incident and were not reported to DHS:

a. Eight incidents involved an ambulance transport to the hospital; and

b. Four incidents involved a police response to the program due to elopement.

The license holder also provides substance use disorder treatment services to youth under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 245G, in addition to Minnesota Rules, chapter 2960. Internal incident reports did not clearly identify which standards were applicable to each youth.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the reporting of Critical Incidents to DHS meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit an incident reporting policy outlining the program’s procedure to ensure the reporting of critical incidents for CRF residents to demonstrate compliance.

5. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 5, item D.

Violation: The license holder’s use of time-out did not meet requirements. Incident report documentation reviewed indicated the use of time-out was implemented on January 2, February 6, and February 26, 2023, and April 20 and August 29, 2022, and August 11, 2021.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the use of time-out meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a policy outlining how time-out will be used to demonstrate compliance.

6. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0140, subpart 1, item A.

Violation: The license holder’s satisfaction survey did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. The license holder did not attempt to survey the resident’s parents/guardians or referring agency regarding their satisfaction with services; and

b. The survey did not include the following required components:

1. Sleeping accommodations;

2. The accuracy, usefulness, and appropriateness of the screening and assessment of the resident’s physical and emotional well-being and functioning;

3. Support of the resident’s regular and special education, related services, and support for implementing the resident’s individual education plan;

4. Support of obtaining needed dental services identified in the resident’s screening and assessments or otherwise observed or reported by staff or other persons involved with the resident’s care; and

5. Support of family reintegration, if appropriate.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that satisfaction surveys meet all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

Personnel Files

7. Citation: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.041, subdivision 5.

Violation: Five of five personnel files reviewed for requirements governing documentation did not meet requirements. The first date the staff person began working in the facility or setting was not documented (personnel files numbered 1 through 5).

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the documentation of the first date of working in a facility or setting meets all applicable requirements.

8. Citation: Minnesota Statutes, section 245C.04, subdivision 7.

Violation: Four of nine personnel files reviewed for requirements governing background studies (personnel files numbered 6 through 9) did not meet requirements. A legal name change occurred during the staff person’s employment; however, the license holder had not yet initiated a new background study or notified the commissioner at the time of the licensing review.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure background studies meet all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

9. Citation: Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0100, subpart 3, item A, and subpart 5.

Violation: Three of three personnel files reviewed for requirements governing orientation training did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. Orientation to the following was not completed before having unsupervised contact with residents (personnel file numbered 4):

1. Emergency procedures, including evacuation routes, emergency telephone numbers, severe storm and tornado procedures, and location of facility alarms and equipment;

2. Cultural diversity and gender sensitivity, culturally specific services, and information about discrimination and racial bias issues;

3. General and special needs, including disability needs of residents and families served;

4. Operational policies and procedures of the license holder including the programs drug and alcohol policy; and

5. Data Practices, regulations, and issues; and

b. Orientation training was not documented with the date, number of hours, and the name of the entity who provided the training in each topic area (personnel files numbered 1, 3, and 4).

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure orientation training meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a revised orientation training checklist template that meets all applicable requirements to demonstrate compliance.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

10. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 14.

Violation: Four of five personnel files reviewed for requirements governing the emergency plan (personnel files numbered 1, 2, 4, and 5) did not meet requirements. The emergency plan was not reviewed with the staff person at least once every six months.

Corrective Action Ordered: Effective immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the review of the emergency plan meets all applicable requirements.

11. Citation: Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0100, subpart 5 and 2960.0150, subpart 4, item B.

Violation: Two of two personnel files reviewed for requirements governing ongoing training did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. Ongoing training was not documented with the date, number of hours, and the name of the entity who provided the training in each topic area (personnel files numbered 2 and 5); and

b. The license holder did not demonstrate the staff person completed at least 24 hours of in-service training for calendar year 2022 (personnel file numbered 2).

Corrective Action Ordered: Effective immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure ongoing training meets all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

Resident Files

12. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0160, subpart 2, item A.

Violation: Three of three resident files reviewed for requirements governing admission criteria (resident files numbered 1 through 3) did not contain documentation of the license holder’s determination of whether the resident is a danger to themselves or others.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the documentation of admission criteria meets all applicable requirements.

13. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0070, subpart 5, items A, B and C.

Violation: Two of three resident files reviewed for requirements governing admission screenings did not meet requirements. The following screens were not completed:

a. Education (resident file numbered 3); and

b. Cultural needs (resident file numbered 2).

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure admission screenings are completed and meet all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

14. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0490, subparts 2 and 3, items B, C, and E.

Violation: Three of three resident files reviewed for requirements governing treatment plans did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. The participation of others was not noted within each plan (resident files numbered 1 through 3);

b. A treatment goal was not developed for dimension three, in which a problem had been identified, for plans dated August 3, 2023 (resident file numbered 1) and March 23, 2023 (resident file numbered 2); and

c. Minimum outcomes to be met before the resident discharged were not included in the plan dated August 3, 2023 (resident file numbered 1).

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the individual treatment plan meets all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

15. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0080, subpart 11, item D.

Violation: Two of three resident files reviewed for requirements governing the safe delivery of medicine (resident files numbered 1 and 3) did not meet requirements. The resident was administered over the counter medications before they were prescribed by a licensed prescriber.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the safe delivery of medicine meets all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

16. Citation: Minnesota Rules, parts 2960.0090, subpart 3 and 2960.0190, subpart 1, item C.

Violation: Two of two resident files reviewed for requirements governing discharge procedures (resident files numbered 2 and 3) did not meet requirements. The license holder’s discharge documentation did not demonstrate:

a. The discrepancies between the resident’s inventoried property turned over to the facility at admission and the property returned to the resident at discharge, along with the resolutions of the discrepancies; and

b. A written notice of discharge was provided to the resident and the residents parents, or legal guardian.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure discharge procedures meet all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

17. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0190, subpart 1, items A and B.

Violation: Two of two resident files reviewed for requirements governing the transition services plan did not meet requirements in the following ways:

a. A transition services plan was not developed (resident file numbered 2);

b. Resources available in the community to meet the resident’s continued needs were not included for all needs identified (resident file numbered 3);

c. Recommendations for how the resident may deal with issues and potential challenges within the environment they were returning to were not included (resident file numbered 3);

d. The plan was not developed with input from the appropriate school district and the continued service provider identified (resident file numbered 3);

e. A copy of the plan was not provided to the continuing treatment service provider that the resident will attend (resident file numbered 3); and

f. The following elements were not included (resident file numbered 3):

1. Recreation and leisure;

2. Appropriate educational, vocational rehabilitation, or training services;

3. Description of the resident’s financial and employment status;

4. Health Services; and

5. Personal Safety Needs.

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure the transition services plan meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of this order, submit a transition service plan for one resident to demonstrate compliance.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

18. Citation: Minnesota Rules, part 2960.0190, subpart 2, items A and B.

Violation: Two of two resident files reviewed for requirements governing discharge documentation did not meet requirements. The discharge summary did not include:

a. The education services, including who provided them, that the resident received during their stay in the program (resident files numbered 2 and 3); and

b. The services, including education that were recommended in the resident’s treatment plan, but were not provided to the resident (resident file numbered 3).

Corrective Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure discharge documentation meets all applicable requirements.

Repeat Violation: The license holder was cited for a similar violation in a Correction Order dated January 29, 2021.

B. Recommendations

The following recommendations are not requirements of Minnesota Rules or laws governing your services or facility. These recommendations are provided to call your attention to areas where your facility is in minimum compliance with the requirements of rules or laws, but it would be advisable to strengthen your efforts in these areas.

1. It is recommended that the license holder schedule a meeting with their Licensor to discuss variance requirements to address the following:

i. Group size; and

ii. Storage of prescription stock medication.

If you fail to correct the violations specified in the Correction Order within the prescribed timelines the Commissioner may issue an Order of Conditional License or may impose a fine and order other licensing sanctions pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, sections 245A.06 and 245A.07.

Submissions required as part of a corrective action ordered must be sent to your Licensor at:

1. By secure email to: tina.christensen@state.mn.us; or

2. By mail to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Tina Christensen

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

C. Right to Request Reconsideration

If you believe any of the citations are in error, you have the right to request that the Commissioner of Human Services reconsider the parts of the Correction Order that you believe to be in error. The request for reconsideration must be in writing and received by the Commissioner within 20 calendar days after receipt of this report. Your request for reconsideration must be sent to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Legal Unit

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

Please note that a request for reconsideration does not stay any provisions or requirements of the Correction Order. The Commissioner’s disposition of a request for reconsideration is final and not subject to appeal under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 14.

If you have any questions regarding this Correction Order, please contact me as soon as possible.

Tina Christensen, Human Services Senior Licensor

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

651-431-6610

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer

reports.raw_content:
November 21, 2025

Jeannie Thompson, Authorized Agent

Heartland Ranch, Inc.

185 Highway 9 NE

Benson, MN 56215

License Number: 1036870 (CRF)

CORRECTION ORDER

Dear Jeannie:

On November 6, 2025, a certification review of Heartland Ranch, Inc., located at 185 Highway 9 NE Benson, MN 56215, was conducted to determine compliance. This review was conducted to determine compliance with state and federal laws and rules governing the provision of The Family First Prevention Services Act under Minnesota Statues 245A.25. As a result of this certification review a Correction Order is being issued.

LICENSING VIOLATIONS

DHS determined that your program did not follow licensing rules and statutes, as described below.

Personnel

1. Violation: Three of three personnel files reviewed for requirements of orientation training did not meet requirements. There was no documentation to support that the staff received training on the following topics prior to having direct contact with a youth:

a. Concepts of trauma informed care and how to provide services to each youth according to those concepts (personnel files numbered 4, 5, and 6); and

b. Impacts of each youth’s culture, race, gender, and sexual orientation on the youth’s behavioral health and traumatic experiences (personnel files numbered 4, 5, and 6).

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25, subdivision 3, paragraph (h).

Repeat Violation: In a Correction Order that DHS issued on June 7, 2023, you were previously found in violation of this same statute.

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that orientation training meets all applicable requirements. Within 30 days receipt of order, submit documentation of a staff orientation plan that demonstrates compliance with all requirements.

2. Violation: One of six personnel files reviewed for requirements of ongoing training did not meet requirements. There was not documentation to support that staff received training on the impacts of each youth’s gender and sexual orientation on the youth’s behavioral health and traumatic experiences for the calendar year 2025 (personnel file numbered 6).

Statute Violated: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25, subdivision 3, paragraph (h).

Corrective Action Required: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure that ongoing training meets all applicable requirements.

Written Response Required

If you fail to correct the violation(s) specified in the Correction Order within the prescribed time lines the Commissioner may issue an Order of Conditional License or may impose a fine and order other licensing sanctions pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, sections 245A.06 and 245A.07.

Submissions required as part of the corrective action ordered must be sent to your licensor by email at Hannah.Horsch@state.mn.us or by mail:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Hannah Horsch

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

YOUR RIGHT TO REQUEST RECONSIDERATION

You have the right to request reconsideration of this order and the cited violations. Your request must:

· Be in writing

· List each violation you are challenging and identify what is inaccurate or incomplete about the information in the order

· Supply information that is accurate or more complete

· Be made before the deadlines provided below

If you are mailing your request, it must be received by DHS within 20 calendar days from when you received this order. If you do not meet this deadline, you lose your right to request reconsideration. The timeline to appeal began when you received this order. Please send it to:

Office of Inspector General

Legal Counsel’s Office

Attn: Licensing Legal Unit

PO Box 64953

St. Paul, MN 55164-0953

If your request is being personally delivered, it must be received by DHS within 20 calendar days from when you received this order. Please bring it to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

Office of Inspector General, Legal Counsel’s Office - Licensing

444 Lafayette Road North

St. Paul, MN 55155

Legal authority

This action is taken under Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, subdivision 1. The timeline to request reconsideration of the order is provided in Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.06, subdivision 2.

Questions

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, you may contact me at 651-431-6270 or at Hannah.Horsch@state.mn.us.

Sincerely,

Hannah Horsch, Licensor

Licensing Division

Office of Inspector General

PO Box 64242 • Saint Paul, Minnesota • 55164-0242 • An Equal Opportunity and Veteran Friendly Employer

July 12, 2023

Jeannie Thompson

Heartland Ranch, Inc.

189 Highway 9 NE

Benson, MN 56215

License Number: 1036870 (CRF)

CORRECTION ORDER

Dear Jeannie Thompson,

On June 7, 2023, a certification review of Heartland Ranch, Inc, located at 189 Highway 9 NE, Benson, MN 56215, was conducted to determine compliance with state and federal laws and rules governing the provision of The Family First Prevention Services Act under Minnesota Statues 245A.25. As a result of this certification review a Correction Order is being issued.

A. Reason for Correction Order

Pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25, subdivision 8 if the Commissioner of the Department of Human Services (DHS) finds that an applicant or license holder has failed to comply with an applicable law or rule and this failure does not imminently endanger the health, safety, or rights of the persons served by the program, the Commissioner may issue a Correction Order to the applicant or license holder.

The following violation(s) of state or federal laws and rules were determined as a result of the licensing review. Corrective action for each violation is required by Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25 and is hereby ordered by the Commissioner of Human Services.

Personnel Files

1. Citation: Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25, subdivisions 3, paragraph (h) and 5, paragraph (f).

Violation: One of five personnel files reviewed for the requirements governing orientation (personnel file numbered 2) did not meet requirements. The file did not contain documentation the staff was trained on the following prior to direct contact:

a. Concepts of trauma-informed care and how to provide services to each youth according to those concepts; and

b. Human trafficking training.

Correction Action Ordered: Immediately and on an ongoing basis, the license holder must ensure orientation training meets all applicable requirements.

If you fail to correct the violations specified in the Correction Order within the prescribed timelines the Commissioner may rescind certification pursuant to Minnesota Statutes, section 245A.25, subdivision 9.

B. Right to Request Reconsideration

If you believe any of the citations are in error, you have the right to request that the Commissioner of Human Services reconsider the parts of the Correction Order that you believe to be in error. The request for reconsideration must be in writing and received by the Commissioner within 20 calendar days after receipt of this report. Your request for reconsideration must be sent to:

Commissioner, Department of Human Services

ATTN: Legal Unit

Licensing Division

PO Box 64242

St. Paul, MN 55164-0242

Please note that a request for reconsideration does not stay any provisions or requirements of the Correction Order. The Commissioner’s disposition of a request for reconsideration is final and not subject to appeal under Minnesota Statutes, chapter 14.

If you have any questions regarding this Correction Order, please contact Ginny Benson, Human Services Licensor at 651-431-6957, as soon as possible.

Ginny Benson, MH/SUD/CRF Senior Licensor
